questions.explanation: Past tense of 'go' is 'went'.
Use 'since' with a specific point in time.

questions.prompt: I look forward to ___ from you.
questions.explanation: 'Look forward to' is followed by a gerund (-ing).

questions.prompt: If it ___ tomorrow, we will stay at home.
questions.explanation: First conditional: if + present simple, will + verb.
Present continuous tense can be used for future arrangements.
'Wish + could' for imaginary ability.

questions.prompt: The movie was ___ than I expected.
questions.explanation: Comparative: 'more + adjective' for longer words.
'Should have' for past criticism/regret.
'Used to + -ing' for accustomed habits.
'Suggest' is followed by gerund (-ing).

questions.prompt: I have lived here ___ five years.
questions.explanation: Use 'for' with a duration of time.
First conditional: 'if + present, will + verb'.

questions.prompt: This is the ___ restaurant in the city.
questions.explanation: Superlative: 'the + -est' for short adjectives.

questions.prompt: I enjoy ___ books in my free time.
questions.explanation: 'Enjoy' is followed by gerund (-ing).
'Deny' is followed by gerund (-ing).
'Should' is followed by base verb.
'Look forward to' is always followed by the gerund (-ing form). The 'to' is a preposition, not part of the infinitive.
'Let' is followed by object + base verb (no 'to').
'Regret' + gerund for something you did.
'Need' + -ing (passive meaning).

questions.prompt: I ___ to Paris twice in my life.
questions.explanation: Present perfect for experiences: 'have/has + past participle'.
Present perfect for actions continuing from past to present.

questions.prompt: When I was young, I ___ play outside every day.
questions.explanation: 'Used to + infinitive' for past habits that no longer happen.

questions.prompt: I want ___ a new car next year.
questions.explanation: 'Want' is followed by infinitive (to + verb).

questions.prompt: You ___ wear a seatbelt in the car.
questions.explanation: 'Must' for obligation and rules.

questions.prompt: We ___ leave now if we want to catch the train.
questions.explanation: 'Have to' for external obligation.

questions.prompt: This book is ___ than that one.
questions.explanation: Comparative form: 'more + adjective' for longer words.

questions.prompt: She is ___ student in the class.
questions.explanation: Superlative: 'the + best' (irregular form).

questions.prompt: Look at those clouds! It ___ rain.
questions.explanation: 'Going to' for predictions based on present evidence.

questions.prompt: I think I ___ stay home tonight.
questions.explanation: 'Will' for instant decisions or predictions.
Present simple for schedules and timetables.

questions.prompt: She ___ very hard at the moment.
questions.explanation: Present continuous for actions happening now.

questions.prompt: I have been studying English ___ three months.
questions.explanation: Use 'for' with a duration of time.
Use 'since' with a starting point in time.
Present perfect with 'yet' for unfinished actions.
Present perfect with 'already' for completed actions.
Present perfect with 'lately' for recent continuing situations.

questions.prompt: We ___ for two hours when the phone rang.
questions.explanation: Past perfect continuous for an action that was ongoing before another past action.
'Afford' is followed by infinitive (to + verb).

questions.prompt: My father made me ___ my room.
questions.explanation: 'Make' is followed by object + base verb (no 'to').
